title: What travels down
checkLabels.t6: The field uses the page font
checkLabels.t8: The field text is normal size
checkLabels.t9: The link in the dark block is white
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: One new rule and two lines in an existing one.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you work only in styles.css.
why.no-hero: There is no element with the hero class.
why.no-hero-link: There is no link in the dark block.
why.input-font: The field still uses a foreign font.
hint2.input-font: Fields and buttons do NOT inherit the font. They have their own, set by the browser.
hint3.input-font: There is a keyword that says: take the value from the parent, whatever it is.
why.button-font: The button still uses a foreign font.
why.input-size: The text size in the field does not match the page.
hint2.input-size: Font and size are two separate properties. Both need the same treatment.
why.link-blue: The link in the dark block is still blue.
hint2.link-blue: The browser stylesheet gives it a colour of its own, which beats inheritance — it does not inherit because it already has a set colour.
hint3.link-blue: Blue on dark brown is below any contrast threshold. Here the right colour is the one of the text around it.
hint4.link-blue: color: inherit; in a rule for links in the block.
why.hero-text: The text in the dark block is no longer white.
why.body-color: The body text colour has changed.
blocks.0.text: Two things on this page behave oddly. The field and the button use a different font, even though body is set to Georgia. And the link in the dark block is blue on dark brown and barely visible.
blocks.2.text: Set a colour on body and it reaches every paragraph inside. Set a border and it stays on body alone. That is not arbitrary — the split follows common sense:
blocks.3.items.0: Text things travel down: colour, font, size, line height, alignment, visibility, cursor.
blocks.3.items.1: Box things do not: padding, margin, border, background, width, display.
blocks.3.items.2: Otherwise every parent border would repeat around every paragraph inside it.
blocks.5.text: The first is fields and buttons. They do NOT inherit the font — the browser stylesheet gives them their own, usually the system one. That is why a form in the middle of a page with careful typography looks pasted in from outside. One word fixes it:
blocks.7.text: The second is links. They do not inherit colour, because the browser has set one for them. Over a dark background that means blue on brown — unreadable. The same word solves this case too.
blocks.8.text: inherit does not mean be inherited. It means take whatever the parent has, whatever that is.
blocks.10.text: Besides inherit there are a few more keywords that work on every property:
blocks.11.items.0: initial — the initial value from the specification, whatever is around it. Careful: for display that is inline, not block.
blocks.11.items.1: unset — the smart mix: behaves like inherit for inherited properties and like initial for the rest.
blocks.11.items.2: revert — restores the value the browser would have given. Usually more useful than initial, because it gives back the sensible value rather than the bare one.
blocks.13.text: Make the field and the button use the page font and size. And make the link in the dark block take the colour of the text around it.
